full_content:
Requirements-collection hook implementation contract: file location, I/O schema, classifier model bounds, retrieval interface, and test coverage

The requirements-collection hook implementation MUST conform to this machine-checkable contract:

LOCATION (binding):
- Hook script: `.claude/hooks/requirements-collector.py` (single canonical path; no alternate locations).
- Hook MUST be registered as `UserPromptSubmit` in `.claude/settings.json`.
- For Codex parity (per .claude/rules/file-bridge-protocol.md and existing `.codex/hooks.json` pattern), the same hook MUST be registered in `.codex/hooks.json` (executable when Codex hook parity becomes live on Windows per ADR-CODEX-HOOK-PARITY-FALLBACK-001).

I/O SCHEMA (binding):
- INPUT: stdin JSON per Claude Code UserPromptSubmit hook contract:
    {
      "session_id": "<S330 etc>",
      "transcript_path": "<absolute path to current transcript>",
      "cwd": "<absolute path>",
      "hook_event_name": "UserPromptSubmit",
      "prompt": "<the full owner message text>"
    }
- OUTPUT: stdout JSON per Claude Code UserPromptSubmit hook output:
    {
      "hookSpecificOutput": {
        "hookEventName": "UserPromptSubmit",
        "additionalContext": "<context block to inject into the agent's next turn — see additionalContext schema below>"
      }
    }
  No other output fields. Errors written to stderr; non-zero exit causes the user prompt to fail with a visible error.

ADDITIONAL_CONTEXT SCHEMA (binding):
The `additionalContext` string MUST be a markdown block with these sections, in this order:

  ## Requirements Collection Hook — Classification

  classification: <requirement_candidate | clarification | decision | chat>
  confidence: <float in [0.0, 1.0]>
  classifier_model: <e.g., "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001">
  classifier_latency_ms: <integer>

  ## Owner statement (verbatim)

  > <the full prompt text, blockquoted line-by-line>

  ## Required agent behavior

  <only present when classification == requirement_candidate>
  <one of:>
    - "Surface the candidate to owner; build 3 retrieval-augmented options per GOV-REQUIREMENTS-COLLECTION-HOOK-001 STEP 2; present via AskUserQuestion; capture as DELIB on selection."
    - "Surface the candidate to owner; the requirement is unambiguous per escape-hatch criteria; present single proposed interpretation via AskUserQuestion (justification: <reason>)."

  ## Retrieval candidates (when classification == requirement_candidate)

  <list of up to 10 retrieval results, each with:>
    - source: <DA | chat | LO_advisory>
    - id_or_locator: <DELIB ID | turn number | report filename>
    - relevance_score: <float in [0.0, 1.0]>
    - excerpt: <up to 200 chars verbatim>

CLASSIFIER MODEL BOUNDS (binding):
- Model: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 OR equivalent (lightweight, < 1B effective parameters for cost containment).
- Token budget per classification: ≤ 500 input + ≤ 100 output (hook MUST truncate excessive prompts and log truncation).
- Timeout: ≤ 5 seconds wall clock; on timeout, hook MUST fall back to classification == "chat" with confidence = 0.0 and emit a warning in additionalContext.
- Cost cap: hook MUST track per-session classification cost and surface it in `additionalContext` for transparency.

RETRIEVAL INTERFACE (binding):
- Deliberation Archive search: MUST use `KnowledgeDB.search_deliberations` (or equivalent CLI) with both semantic and structured filters.
- Chat retrieval: MUST read from `transcript_path` (path provided in hook input); read last N turns (N configurable via env var `GTKB_REQUIREMENTS_HOOK_CHAT_TURN_LIMIT`, default 50).
- LO advisory retrieval: MUST scan `independent-progress-assessments/CODEX-INSIGHT-DROPBOX/INSIGHTS-*.md` and rank by topical relevance to the owner statement (text-similarity score acceptable).
- All retrievals MUST complete within 10 seconds wall clock total; on timeout, the hook MUST emit available results with a "partial retrieval" warning.

TEST COVERAGE (binding):
- A regression test MUST exist at `tests/scripts/test_requirements_collection_hook.py` (or equivalent path under the GT-KB framework once Agent Red migrates) verifying:
    (a) classifier returns valid label for each of {imperative, descriptive, structural, comparative, inferred, chat} fixture inputs;
    (b) retrieval queries each of the 3 sources;
    (c) escape-hatch fires correctly for unambiguous cases;
    (d) hook output schema validates against the additionalContext schema above;
    (e) cost cap and timeout enforced.

DOCTOR INVARIANTS (testable):
- `gt project doctor` MUST verify:
  (a) hook script exists at the canonical path;
  (b) hook is registered in `.claude/settings.json` UserPromptSubmit section;
  (c) classifier model is in the approved-model allowlist;
  (d) regression test file exists at the canonical test path.

Rationale:
This DCL turns GOV-REQUIREMENTS-COLLECTION-HOOK-001's requirements into a machine-enforceable implementation contract. Without fixed schema, model bounds, and retrieval interfaces, downstream sessions would re-invent the hook's shape each time and risk inconsistent behavior. The contract makes the hook auditable + drift-resistant.

Owner approval: Approve as drafted (S330 chat, 2026-05-04, via AskUserQuestion).
explicit_change_request: Owner approved insertion via AskUserQuestion: "Approve as drafted". This artifact spawns from DELIB-S330-REQUIREMENTS-COLLECTION-HOOK-WITH-3-OPTION-CLARIFICATION. Source intent: Implementation contract for GOV-REQUIREMENTS-COLLECTION-HOOK-001; specifies hook location, I/O schema, classifier bounds, retrieval interface, test coverage.
change_reason: S330 spawn-queue item: DCL-REQUIREMENTS-COLLECTION-HOOK-CONTRACT-001 created from DELIB-S330-REQUIREMENTS-COLLECTION-HOOK-WITH-3-OPTION-CLARIFICATION per just-approved DELIB-S330-SPEC-CAPTURE-TRANSPARENCY transparency rule.
